Farn PolrrrcAI- PRACTTcES C ouurssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 958i2-0807
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
August 20,2002
Stephanie Campbell
1408 Virginia Avenue
Bakersfield, CA 93307
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No.W-02-222
Dear Ms. Campbell
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r
You previously served on the City of Bakersfield Southeast Project Area
Committee ("SEPAC"), a volunteer citizen's advisory committee providing input to the
Bakersfield Redevelopment Agency conceming redevelopment of the southeastern area
of the city of Bakersfield ("city"). During the time you served on SEpAC, you owned
and ran a small business in the City, called the Comerstone Group. You state that your
business was solicited by City officials to provide outreach services to the southeastern
Bakersfield community concerning public participation in redevelopment planning. you
are seeking our advice regarding the City's concerns that this contract presents a conflict
of interest and a violation of Government Code section 1090.2
Your request pertains to a private contract executed between the City and. a
business that you own. As such, your request does not present a question under the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act, which provisions address the question of
whether a public official's involvement in governmental decisions will have a
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect upon his or her economic interests:
"No public official at any level of state or local govemment shall make,
participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official position to
t Govern-"rt Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18-997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 The conflict-of-interest provisioni of the Act and of Government Code sections 1090, et seq., are
two distinct statutory schemes, each imposing its own compliance obligations. For information regarding
Government Code sections 1090, et seq., please consult with the state Attorney General's office.


i
File No. W-02-222
Page No. 2
influence a govemmental decision in which he knows or has reason to
know he has a financial interest." (Section 87100.)
In addition, to the extent the Political Reform Act may be implicated, your request
concerns past conduct. The Commission does not offer public officials or former public
officials advice concerning past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8XA), copy enclosed.)
Enclosed for your information is a copy of our publication, "Can I Vote?" which
describes our standard eight-step of conflict-of-interest analysis (regulations 18701-
18708).
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel

sections.question: Whether a contract between the City of Bakersfield and a business owned by a former SEPAC member (who provided outreach services during her volunteer service on the committee) presents a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act and Government Code section 1090.
sections.conclusion: The request does not present a question under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act because the Act addresses whether a public official's involvement in governmental decisions will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect upon their economic interests. Additionally, the Commission does not offer advice concerning past conduct.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a private contract between a city and a business owned by a former volunteer committee member, where the business was solicited to provide services related to the committee's area of focus, constitute a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the request falls outside the scope of the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions, as the Act applies to governmental decision-making affecting an official's financial interests, not private contracts. Furthermore, the FPPC declines to advise on past conduct.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a proper advice letter with clear identification of the question and FPPC's conclusion. The letter declines to provide the requested advice on substantive grounds rather than providing an affirmative answer. Past conduct restriction cited in Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a contract between the City of Bakersfield and a business owned by a former SEPAC member (who provided outreach services during her volunteer service on the committee) presents a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act and Government Code section 1090.

CONCLUSION: The request does not present a question under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act because the Act addresses whether a public official's involvement in governmental decisions will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect upon their economic interests. Additionally, the Commission does not offer advice concerning past conduct.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to a request from a former Bakersfield city advisory committee member regarding whether a contract between the city and her business for outreach services constitutes a conflict of interest. The FPPC declines to advise, finding the issue does not implicate the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions and involves past conduct outside the scope of advisory opinions.
